---
title: "get total video duration resursive?"
date: 2012-10-25
forum: General Help
---

### Post by sohlinux on 2012-10-25
Hi
Is there any way of getting the total video duration recursive using ffmpeg or similar? I have a folder and sub folders of videos, avis, wmv mpg etc, I want to get the total time for all videos, there are 1600+ so I dont want to add them all up individually.

thanks

---

### Post by Vaphell on 2012-10-25
try this
```
#!/bin/bash

videos=( "$HOME/Downloads" "$HOME/Movies" )
default_ext=( mp4 avi )
dump_file=./video_times.txt

# parameters or default values?
(( $#==0 )) && ext=( "${default_ext[@]}" ) || ext=( "$@" )
echo "locations: ${videos[@]/$HOME/~}"
echo "extensions: ${ext[@]}"

for(( i=0; i<${#ext[@]}; i++ ))
do 
  (( i>0 )) && find_params+=( -o )
  find_params+=( -iname '*.'${ext[$i]} )
done
find_params=( '(' "${find_params[@]}" ')' )

rm "$dump_file" 2>/dev/null

while IFS= read -rd '' f
do
  echo "$(( ++n )): $f"
  # time in original HH:MM:SS.ms format and in SSSSSS.ms
  ffmpeg -i "$f" 2>&1 | awk '/Duration:/ { split($2, t, ":|,"); print $2 t[1]*3600+t[2]*60+t[3]; }' >> "$dump_file"
done < <( find "${videos[@]}" "${find_params[@]}" -print0 )

awk -F, '    {
               total+=$2;
         #  printing numbers during work
         #      printf("%+10.2f\t(%10.2f)\n", $2, total );
             }
         END {
         #      print "-----------------------------";
               printf( "%.2f seconds in %d files\n", total, NR );
               h=total/3600; total%=3600;
               m=total/60; total%=60;
               printf( "%02d:%02d:%s\n", h, m, total )
             }' "$dump_file"

```

it could be done with shorter code but i added some configurability aimed at ease of use.
Paths and default extensions are conveniently at the top, also the script 
It is possible to count time right off the bat, but i chose dumping times to a file so i could have all the time in the world to work with the output data.

```
$ ./total.sh
locations: ~/Downloads
extensions: mp4 avi
...
100340.88 seconds in 52 files
27:52:20.88

$ ./total.sh mp4
locations: ~/Downloads
extensions: mp4
...
52897.06 seconds in 18 files
14:41:37.06

$ ./total.sh wmv avi
locations: ~/Downloads
extensions: wmv avi
...
47443.82 seconds in 34 files
13:10:43.82

```

### Post by Vaphell on 2012-10-25
the last thing the script does is to run awk to process that file, doesn't it do that?
either way if you copy paste the whole awk code and use the name of the dump file it should produce the result

and it's
```
00:16:17.79   ,   977.79
```
time given by ffmpeg and the same time in seconds
i guess i will rework it to clearly separate both values. That first one is not necessary but it doesn't hurt anybody and it could help with tracking issues, should they arise.


EDIT: try this
```

videos=( "$HOME/Downloads" "$HOME/Movies" )
default_ext=( mp4 avi )
dump_file=./video_times.txt

# parameters or default values?
(( $#==0 )) && ext=( "${default_ext[@]}" ) || ext=( "$@" )
echo "locations: ${videos[@]/$HOME/~}"
echo "extensions: ${ext[@]}"

for(( i=0; i<${#ext[@]}; i++ ))
do 
  (( i>0 )) && find_params+=( -o )
  find_params+=( -iname '*.'${ext[$i]} )
done
find_params=( '(' "${find_params[@]}" ')' )

rm "$dump_file" 2>/dev/null

while IFS= read -rd '' f
do
  echo "$(( ++n )): $f"
  # time in SSSSSS.ms format and in original HH:MM:SS.ms
  t=$( ffmpeg -i "$f" 2>&1 | awk '/Duration:/ {
                                                sub(/,/,"",$2 );
                                                split($2, t, ":|");
                                                printf( "%10.2f\t%s", t[1]*3600+t[2]*60+t[3], $2 );
                                              }' )
  printf "%s\t%s\n" "$t" "$f" >> "$dump_file"
done < <( find "${videos[@]}" "${find_params[@]}" -print0 )

# calculate result
awk '    {
           total+=$1;
     #  printing numbers during work
     #      printf("%+10.2f\t(%10.2f)\n", $1, total );
         }
     END {
     #      print "-----------------------------";
           printf( "%.2f seconds in %d files\n", total, NR );
           h=total/3600; total%=3600;
           m=total/60; total%=60;
           printf( "%02d:%02d:%05.2f\n", h, m, total )
         }' "$dump_file"
             

```
